stories.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
stories.description: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.

stories.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
stories.description: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.

stories.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
stories.description: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

stories.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
stories.description: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

stories.title: Iran rejects U.S. peace plan. And, jury finds Meta, Google to blame in addiction trial
stories.description: Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.

stories.title: Trump grants Iran another extension on a deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: President Trump said he'll hold off on striking Iran's power plants until April 6, postponing a deadline for Iran to let ships transit safely through the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
stories.description: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.

stories.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
stories.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.

stories.title: Greetings from Turkey's border with Iran, where Iranians let loose on the dance floor
stories.description: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.

stories.title: Trump's Iran strategy is to pursue two off-ramps at once
stories.description: The White House insists the president is dictating events, but even some Republicans are frustrated by mixed messaging about his war aims.

stories.title: Zelensky visits Saudi Arabia after offering Ukraine's drone expertise
stories.description: There is renewed urgency for Ukraine to get a drone deal done, as the US increasingly focuses on Iran.

stories.title: Iran's rejection of US talks reflects deep mistrust
stories.description: This does not mean the door is shut, as Iran's tough stance may be more about setting conditions than rejecting diplomacy.

stories.title: Iran's foreign minister says there are no negotiations with US
stories.description: Trump says he is talking to "the right people" in Iran, but Tehran says no such negotiations are being held.

stories.title: 'There's no safety anymore': Palestinians warn of expanding West Bank settler violence
stories.description: There has been a surge of attacks by Israeli settlers on Palestinian villages across the occupied territory since the start of the Iran war.

stories.title: Small window open for US-Iran talks, but swift end to war still unlikely
stories.description: President Trump says the US is already dealing with a "top person" in Iran, but Tehran denies that any talks have begun.

stories.title: What Iranians make of the possibility of talks to end the war
stories.description: Some Iranians dismiss the possibility of talks to end the war, others worry a deal may leave the current leaders in place.

stories.title: Iran war shows norms of international conflicts have been overturned
stories.description: US threats against energy infrastructure, and Iran's retaliation on its Gulf neighbours, signal a clear change.

stories.title: A committed pharmacist and a homesick blogger – the Iranian civilians killed in the war
stories.description: The toll on civilians is mounting fast as the US-Israeli war on Iran continues.

stories.title: US' and Iran's options for ending war narrow the longer it goes on
stories.description: Iran shows no sign of climbdown, while air power alone cannot achieve the US' main goal.

stories.title: Israel to expand ground and air attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon
stories.description: There are fears in Lebanon that Israel is preparing for a large-scale ground invasion.

stories.title: Trump at a crossroads as US weighs tough options in Iran
stories.description: Statements from President Trump that the war is almost over are not matching up with the reality on the ground.

stories.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
stories.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran on 28 February.

stories.title: Why does the US have Iran's Kharg Island in its sights?
stories.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

stories.title: 'You can't smell Nowruz in the air': Iran marks Persian new year under threat of strikes
stories.description: Iranians speak to the BBC about preparing for the Nowruz new year festival during the war.

stories.title: Middle East live: Iran launches strikes on Israel and Gulf sites
stories.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Friday they conducted missile and drone strikes the previous day against targets in Israel and US-linked military facilities across the Gulf, hitting sites in the UAE, Qatar, Kuwait and Bahrain, including a Patriot air-defence maintenance facil...

stories.title: US judge temporarily halts Trump-era sanctions on AI firm Anthropic
stories.description: A US federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked sanctions imposed by the Trump administration on AI company Anthropic, saying the measures likely violated the law. The ruling freezes a Pentagon designation of the firm as a national security risk, protecting it while the govern...

stories.title: Iran war is starting to disrupt global value chains
stories.description: Concern that the Iran war could plunge the world into stagflation is rising as hostilities in the Middle East continue with no end in sight. The European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD)'s chief economist Beata Javorcik tells France 24's Yuka Royer that while the wo...

stories.title: War in the Mideast: are the UK, Australia and Taiwan about to run out of fuel?
stories.description: As Iran’s disruption of the Strait of Hormuz fuels global energy anxiety, viral posts claim that major economies are on the brink of an unprecedented crisis. From footage of endless car queues in the UK to alarming graphics about Australia and Taiwan’s dwindling reserves, social ...

stories.title: Trump again berates NATO for inaction in securing Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Donald Trump lambasted NATO and other allies for rejecting his appeals to help secure the Strait of Hormuz, through which around a fifth of global crude oil and liquefied natural gas normally passes. "I'm so disappointed in NATO, because this was a test for NATO" the US President...

stories.title: Israeli strike kills Iran's navy commander Alireza Tangsiri
stories.description: Israel's Defence Minister Israel Katz announced on Thursday that an air strike killed the commander of the Iranian Revolutionary Guards' navy, Alireza Tangsiri. The US military confirmed the operation and said Tangsiri's death "makes the region safer".

stories.title: Trump insists Iran is ‘begging to make a deal’ after Tehran dismisses ceasefire plan
stories.description: A day after Tehran dismissed Donald Trump’s 15-point ceasefire plan, the American leader claimed that Iran was “begging to make a deal,” and that he wasn’t the one pushing for negotiations. Reporting from Tehran for FRANCE 24, Reza Sayah says that Iranian leaders do not consider ...

stories.title: Winding down or spinning out of control? Trump's mixed signals on Iran rattle traditional allies
stories.description: As G-Seven foreign ministers root for the offramp in the Iran war, François Picard’s panel weighs the damage done to the region, to the world economy and to transatlantic trust. From Donald Trump’s threats against NATO allies to France denying it disinvited South Africa from next...

stories.title: As 'war of choice' escalates into international crisis, Miller says Trump has 'no strategy'
stories.description: Genie Godula welcomes Aaron David Miller, former State Department Middle East negotiator, and Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace. According to Miller, Trump's war on Iran is evolving into systemic entrapment, in which regional dynamics involving Iran,...

stories.title: 'Tehran assumes war is behind them: Iran will not capitulate, Trump has it all wrong'
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Danny Citrinowicz, Senior Researcher in the Iran and Shi'ite Axis Program at the Institute for National Security Studies. According to Citrinowicz, the trajectory of the war in Iran hinges not on battlefield dominance alone, but on competing per...

stories.title: Iran's chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz: Fertiliser supply chains under strain
stories.description: As tensions between the US, Israel and Iran choke the Strait of Hormuz, cutting global energy and fertiliser flows, markets face rising prices and food insecurity. We discuss the global fallout with Carlos Razo from the UN Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD), exploring t...

stories.title: Russia says report it is sending drones to Iran 'lies'
stories.description: The Kremlin said Thursday that a report in the Financial Times that it was close to completing a shipment of drones to Iran was "lies". Russia is a close ally of Iran, but denies having sent military aid to its partner since the United States and Israel began attacking the countr...

stories.title: Trump insists Iran operations 'extremely' ahead of schedule
stories.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that the Iran war was "extremely" ahead of schedule, as he pushed Tehran to make a deal at his first cabinet meeting since the conflict began nearly a month ago. As Tehran publicly rebuffed negotiations, Trump again cited the original timef...

stories.title: US claims 'strong signs' for deal with Iran
stories.description: Speaking at a cabinet meeting, the #Trump administration claimed there were strong signs a #deal could soon be reached with #Iran for a #ceasefire.

stories.title: The Strait of Hormuz is not just an oil chokepoint
stories.description: It is the single point of failure of globalised production.

stories.title: Sri Lanka braces for new economic crisis as war on Iran continues
stories.description: For many Sri Lankans, the fuel shortage triggered by the war carries echoes of the 2022 economic crisis - with a twist.

stories.title: Malaysia’s ships allowed to pass Strait of Hormuz, PM Anwar says
stories.description: Malaysian leader says oil tankers granted clearance by Iran as government introduces measures to conserve fuel.

stories.title: First responders rescue man from Tehran building hit by air strike
stories.description: The Iranian Red Crescent Society released video showing rescuers in Tehran pulling a man from a damaged building.

stories.title: Red Crescent video shows aftermath of strike on homes in Iran’s Urmia
stories.description: Iranian Red Crescent video shows the aftermath of a strike on a residential area in Urmia.

stories.title: Albanese says Australia playing “constructive” role in the war on Iran
stories.description: Australia’s prime minister says his government has made a “constructive” contribution to the war on Iran.

stories.title: Analysts say Trump doesn’t understand Iran reality has changed
stories.description: Watch this discussion between Ross Harrison and Hassan Ahmadian on US strategy regarding Iran.

stories.title: Iran war live: Trump delays attacks on Iranian energy sector by 10 days
stories.description: Iranian missiles, drones target Kuwait, UAE, Saudi Arabia and Jordan as Trump says Iran has 'chance to make a deal'.

stories.title: Trump ‘pausing’ plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days
stories.description: Donald Trump says he is “pausing” his plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days.

stories.title: EU’s top diplomat urges US to pressure Russia to stop aiding Iran
stories.description: The European Union’s Foreign Policy Chief Kaja Kallas says the US needs to pressure Russia to stop assisting Iran.

stories.title: Manila’s streets empty as fuel prices surge amid Strait of Hormuz crisis
stories.description: Empty streets expose another crisis as Filipinos battle soaring oil prices, dwindling incomes, and a stagnating economy.

stories.title: Why Iraq is the most fragile front in the US-Israel war on Iran
stories.description: Iraq is under attack from all sides as militias and foreign powers clash.

stories.title: ‘Very important neutral party’: Pakistan can play greater diplomatic role, says US lawmaker
stories.description: WASHINGTON: As tensions surrounding Iran once again test regional diplomacy, Pakistan is emerging in Washington’s policy conversations as a potential intermediary — a shift that signals a broader reassessment of its strategic utility beyond the traditional security framework. At ...

stories.title: No let-up in US-Israeli strikes on Iran despite peace push
stories.description: TEHRAN: Protesters carrying flags and placards attend an anti-US and Israel rally, even as bombs continued to fall on the Iranian capital.—Reuters • Staff safe as neighbourhood housing Pakistan embassy comes under heavy bombardment• Tabriz, Bandar Abbas, Mashhad, Birjand hit; Ira...

stories.title: Houthis ready to join Iran war ‘if needed’
stories.description: ADEN/GENEVA: Yemen’s Iran-aligned Houthi movement, whose attacks on the Red Sea caused international shipping and trade chaos during the Gaza war, stands ready to strike the key waterway again in solidarity with Tehran, one Houthi leader said, a move that would deepen a global oi...

stories.title: US public unease grows over Iran war as polls show majority disapprove of Trump
stories.description: https://www.dawn.com/news/1985719

stories.title: US envoy to UK warns against cancelling king’s visit
stories.description: LONDON: Washington’s envoy to the UK said on Thursday it would be a “big mistake” to cancel King Charles III’s planned state visit to the US, following calls to abandon the trip over the Middle East war. It has been rumoured for months that the British monarch will visit the Unit...

stories.title: Global economy struggles to deal with trade disruptions
stories.description: • GDP growth projected to slow to 2.9pc in 2026, rise to 3.0pc in 2027• WTO chief urges overhaul of global trade system• G7 foreign ministers meet near Paris to discuss war• Asian countries, including Vietnam, Thailand and Sri Lanka, turn to Russian oil PARIS: The escalating conf...

stories.title: US judge questions block on Maduro’s use of Venezuelan funds for defence
stories.description: A US judge questioned on Thursday whether the US can bar Venezuela from funding Nicolas Maduro’s legal defence without violating his US constitutional rights, but stopped short of dismissing drug-trafficking charges against the ousted Venezuelan president facing trial in New York...

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
